artifacts.title: Tiredness and fatigue
assertions: Persistent unexplained fatigue, functional impact, weight loss, mood change or sleep-related gasping warrant clinical assessment.
Sleep, lifestyle, stress, mood, hormonal change, illness, treatment and medicines are relevant contexts.
Associated palpitations, dyspnoea, pallor, thirst, urinary frequency, weight loss and thyroid-like symptoms inform evaluation but do not establish a diagnosis.

artifacts.title: ME/CFS: diagnosis and management
assertions: Persistent debilitating fatigue, post-exertional malaise, unrefreshing sleep and cognitive difficulty with reduced activity are characteristic features requiring assessment when unexplained.
Assessment must consider medical, mental-health and social context and exclude other explanations; the interview must not assign ME/CFS.

artifacts.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral — fatigue
assertions: Persistent fatigue in adults and unexplained fatigue with smoking, asbestos exposure, cough, dyspnoea, chest pain, weight loss or appetite loss can require investigation.
The interview records these features and age context without diagnosing cancer.

artifacts.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management
assertions: Mood assessment considers duration, function, sleep, lifestyle, stressors, medicines, alcohol and substance use.
People with depressive symptoms should be asked directly about suicidal ideation and intent; considerable immediate risk requires urgent specialist help.

artifacts.title: STOM SNOMED CT lookup and MRCM allowed attributes for fatigue
assertions: STOM confirmed active 84229001 Fatigue and 248274002 Lack of energy concepts.
Finding site and Severity were allowed for both concepts.
MRCM validates terminology modeling only and does not control clinical questions, diagnoses, priority or safety.
